message:
✨ Add OpenEverest fix missions for common issues (#1973)

Add 5 fix missions sourced from openeverest/openeverest issues:
- #1664: Installation stuck with ImagePullBackOff
- #1665: Uninstallation stuck with ImagePullBackOff
- #1568: Upgrade to 1.8.0 fails with empty namespace error
- #1569: OIDC not working with Azure AD
- #1909: Cannot create PostgreSQL cluster with ClusterIP type

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "openeverest-1568-everest-1-8-0-upgrade-error",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "openeverest: Everest 1.8.0 upgrade error — empty namespace may not be set",
+    "description": "Upgrading OpenEverest to 1.8.0 fails with 'an empty namespace may not be set when a resource name is provided' and 'could not retrieve subscription'. This affects both everestctl upgrade and helm upgrade paths.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the upgrade error",
+        "description": "Check the error output when running the upgrade:\n```bash\neverestctl upgrade --dry-run\n```\nIf you see 'an empty namespace may not be set when a resource name is provided', this is a known issue with empty namespace references in the Everest configuration."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Check for empty namespace entries",
+        "description": "List all namespaces managed by Everest and check for empty entries:\n```bash\nkubectl get configmap everest-settings -n everest-system -o yaml\nkubectl get namespace -l app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=everest\n```\nLook for any empty or malformed namespace references."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix",
+        "description": "Clean up any empty namespace references in the Everest configuration before upgrading:\n```bash\nkubectl edit configmap everest-settings -n everest-system\n```\nRemove any empty string entries from the namespace list.\n\nIf the Helm upgrade hangs with 'context canceled' due to a pre-upgrade hook, delete the stuck hook job:\n```bash\nkubectl delete job -l app.kubernetes.io/component=pre-upgrade-hook -n everest-system\n```\n\nThen retry the upgrade:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade everest-core percona/everest --namespace everest-system\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm the upgrade succeeded",
+        "description": "Verify the upgrade completed successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n everest-system\nhelm list -n everest-system\neverestctl version\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The upgrade fails due to empty namespace references in the Everest configuration. Remove empty namespace entries from the everest-settings configmap, delete any stuck pre-upgrade hook jobs, then retry the upgrade.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get configmap everest-settings -n everest-system -o yaml",
+        "kubectl delete job -l app.kubernetes.io/component=pre-upgrade-hook -n everest-system",
+        "helm upgrade everest-core percona/everest --namespace everest-system"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "openeverest",
+      "troubleshoot",
+      "upgrade",
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "ConfigMap",
+      "Job"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest/issues/1568",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest"
+    },
+    "reactions": 0,
+    "comments": 7,
+    "synthesizedBy": "kubestellar-bot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.27",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "helm",
+      "everestctl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A Kubernetes cluster with OpenEverest installed, attempting to upgrade to 1.8.0+."
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "openeverest-1569-oidc-does-not-work-with-azure",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "openeverest: OIDC does not work with Azure AD — internal error after login",
+    "description": "OIDC authentication with Azure AD allows login (user name is shown) but the Everest interface displays an internal error. This affects users configuring Azure AD as their OIDC provider.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the OIDC error",
+        "description": "Check if the Everest UI shows an internal error after OIDC login:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=100 | grep -i oidc\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for token validation errors or scope-related issues."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review OIDC configuration",
+        "description": "Check the current OIDC settings:\n```bash\nkubectl get configmap everest-settings -n everest-system -o yaml\n```\n\nFor Azure AD, the configuration should look like:\n```\neverestctl settings oidc configure \\\n    --issuer-url=\"https://login.microsoftonline.com/<TENANT_ID>/v2.0\" \\\n    --client-id=\"<APP_CLIENT_ID>\" \\\n    --scopes=\"openid,profile,email\"\n```\n\nNote: Avoid using `<app-id>/.default` scope as it can cause issues with token validation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify Azure AD app registration",
+        "description": "In the Azure portal, verify your app registration:\n1. Ensure the app is registered as a SPA (Single Page Application)\n2. Redirect URIs include your Everest URL\n3. Token configuration includes 'email' optional claim\n4. API permissions include: openid, profile, email\n\nCheck that the token contains the expected claims:\n```bash\n# Decode the JWT token to inspect claims\necho '<token>' | cut -d'.' -f2 | base64 -d 2>/dev/null | jq .\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm OIDC is working",
+        "description": "After fixing the configuration, verify OIDC login works end-to-end:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=50 --since=5m | grep -i oidc\n```\nConfirm there are no more error messages after login."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "OIDC with Azure AD fails because of scope configuration or missing token claims. Ensure the Azure AD app registration is configured as a SPA with the correct redirect URIs and that the OIDC scopes are set to 'openid,profile,email' without the .default scope.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "everestctl settings oidc configure --issuer-url=\"https://login.microsoftonline.com/<TENANT_ID>/v2.0\" --client-id=\"<APP_CLIENT_ID>\" --scopes=\"openid,profile,email\"",
+        "kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=100 | grep -i oidc"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "openeverest",
+      "troubleshoot",
+      "oidc",
+      "azure",
+      "authentication"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "ConfigMap"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest/issues/1569",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest"
+    },
+    "reactions": 0,
+    "comments": 9,
+    "synthesizedBy": "kubestellar-bot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.27",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "everestctl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A Kubernetes cluster with OpenEverest installed and Azure AD configured as OIDC provider."
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "openeverest-1664-installation-namespace-provisioning-stuck-with-imagepullbackoff",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "openeverest: Installation/namespace provisioning stuck with ImagePullBackOff for the bitnami/kubectl image",
+    "description": "Installation or namespace provisioning is stuck in the 'Provisioning database namespace' step because the everest-operators-installer job cannot pull the bitnami/kubectl image due to Docker Hub rate limits or network restrictions.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the stuck installation",
+        "description": "Check for stuck pods in the everest-system namespace:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n everest-system\nkubectl get pods -l job-name=everest-operators-installer --all-namespaces\n```\nLook for pods in ImagePullBackOff or ErrImagePull state."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Diagnose the image pull failure",
+        "description": "Describe the stuck pod to see the exact image pull error:\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod -l job-name=everest-operators-installer -n everest-system\n```\nLook for events showing rate limiting (429 Too Many Requests) or network timeout errors on the bitnami/kubectl image."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix",
+        "description": "**Option A: Create an image pull secret for Docker Hub**\n```bash\nkubectl create secret docker-registry dockerhub-creds \\\n  --docker-server=https://index.docker.io/v1/ \\\n  --docker-username=<your-username> \\\n  --docker-password=<your-token> \\\n  -n everest-system\n```\n\n**Option B: Mirror the bitnami/kubectl image to your private registry**\n```bash\ndocker pull bitnami/kubectl:latest\ndocker tag bitnami/kubectl:latest <your-registry>/bitnami/kubectl:latest\ndocker push <your-registry>/bitnami/kubectl:latest\n```\nThen update the job to use your mirrored image.\n\n**Option C: Delete the stuck job and retry**\n```bash\nkubectl delete job everest-operators-installer -n everest-system\n```\nThen re-run the installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm the issue is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify that the installation proceeds and pods reach Running state:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n everest-system\nkubectl get jobs -n everest-system\n```\nConfirm that all pods are Running or Completed."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The installation hangs because the everest-operators-installer job fails to pull the bitnami/kubectl image from Docker Hub, typically due to rate limits. The fix is to provide Docker Hub credentials via an image pull secret, mirror the image to a private registry, or retry when rate limits reset.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get pods -l job-name=everest-operators-installer --all-namespaces",
+        "kubectl describe pod -l job-name=everest-operators-installer -n everest-system",
+        "kubectl delete job everest-operators-installer -n everest-system"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "openeverest",
+      "troubleshoot",
+      "imagepull",
+      "docker-hub",
+      "installation"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Job"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest/issues/1664",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest"
+    },
+    "reactions": 0,
+    "comments": 1,
+    "synthesizedBy": "kubestellar-bot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.27",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A Kubernetes cluster where OpenEverest installation is stuck at namespace provisioning."
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "openeverest-1665-uninstallation-stuck-with-imagepullbackoff",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "openeverest: Uninstallation stuck with ImagePullBackOff for the bitnami/kubectl image",
+    "description": "Uninstallation is stuck in the 'Deleting database namespace' step because the everest-helm-pre-delete-hook job cannot pull the bitnami/kubectl image.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the stuck uninstallation",
+        "description": "Check for stuck pre-delete hook pods:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -l job-name -n everest-system | grep pre-delete\nkubectl get jobs -n everest-system | grep pre-delete\n```\nLook for pods in ImagePullBackOff state."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Diagnose the image pull failure",
+        "description": "Describe the stuck pod:\n```bash\nkubectl describe pod -l job-name=everest-helm-pre-delete-hook -n everest-system\n```\nCheck events for Docker Hub rate limiting or network issues with the bitnami/kubectl image."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix",
+        "description": "Delete the stuck pre-delete hook job to allow uninstallation to proceed:\n```bash\nkubectl delete job -l app.kubernetes.io/component=pre-delete-hook -n everest-system\n```\n\nIf the Helm uninstall is still stuck, force it:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall everest-core --namespace everest-system --no-hooks\n```\n\nThen manually clean up remaining resources:\n```bash\nkubectl delete namespace everest-system\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm the uninstallation completed",
+        "description": "Verify that all OpenEverest resources have been removed:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n everest-system\nkubectl get namespace everest-system\nhelm list -n everest-system\n```\nAll commands should return empty or 'not found'."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The uninstallation hangs because the Helm pre-delete hook job fails to pull the bitnami/kubectl image. Delete the stuck job or use helm uninstall with --no-hooks to bypass the hook, then manually clean up the namespace.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl delete job -l app.kubernetes.io/component=pre-delete-hook -n everest-system",
+        "helm uninstall everest-core --namespace everest-system --no-hooks",
+        "kubectl delete namespace everest-system"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "openeverest",
+      "troubleshoot",
+      "uninstall",
+      "imagepull",
+      "helm-hooks"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Job"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest/issues/1665",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest"
+    },
+    "reactions": 0,
+    "comments": 1,
+    "synthesizedBy": "kubestellar-bot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.27",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl",
+      "helm"
+    ],
+    "description": "A Kubernetes cluster where OpenEverest uninstallation is stuck."
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "openeverest-1909-cannot-create-postgres-cluster-with-clusterip",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "openeverest: Cannot create PostgreSQL cluster with ClusterIP expose type",
+    "description": "Creating a PostgreSQL database cluster via the UI fails with 'Unsupported value: ClusterIP: supported values: internal, external'. The UI incorrectly sends 'ClusterIP' as the proxy expose type instead of the expected 'internal' or 'external' values.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the error",
+        "description": "Check for the error when creating a database cluster:\n```bash\nkubectl get events -n <database-namespace> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | grep -i invalid\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=everest-server -n everest-system --tail=50 | grep -i clusterip\n```\nLook for the validation error: `spec.proxy.expose.type: Unsupported value: \"ClusterIP\"`."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the workaround",
+        "description": "Create the database cluster using kubectl with the correct expose type value instead of the UI:\n```bash\ncat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -\napiVersion: everest.percona.com/v1alpha1\nkind: DatabaseCluster\nmetadata:\n  name: <cluster-name>\n  namespace: <namespace>\nspec:\n  engine:\n    type: postgresql\n    version: \"16\"\n    replicas: 1\n    storage:\n      size: 25Gi\n  proxy:\n    type: pgbouncer\n    replicas: 1\n    expose:\n      type: internal\nEOF\n```\n\nUse `internal` for ClusterIP-equivalent behavior (accessible only within the cluster) or `external` for LoadBalancer-equivalent behavior."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the database cluster is created",
+        "description": "Check that the database cluster was created successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get databasecluster -n <namespace>\nkubectl get pods -n <namespace> -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=<cluster-name>\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The OpenEverest UI sends 'ClusterIP' as the proxy expose type but the API only accepts 'internal' or 'external'. Use kubectl to create the cluster with type 'internal' (equivalent to ClusterIP) as a workaround until the UI is fixed.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl get databasecluster -A",
+        "kubectl get events -n <namespace> --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | grep -i invalid"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "openeverest",
+      "troubleshoot",
+      "postgresql",
+      "database",
+      "ui-bug"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "DatabaseCluster",
+      "Pod",
+      "Service"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest/issues/1909",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/openeverest/openeverest"
+    },
+    "reactions": 0,
+    "comments": 2,
+    "synthesizedBy": "kubestellar-bot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.27",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A Kubernetes cluster with OpenEverest installed, attempting to create a PostgreSQL database cluster."
+  }
+}
